question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the pinger's DNS resolver to return NXDOMAIN for consensus node hostnames cause all cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) calls to fail with DNS resolution errors, creating a network partition that is indistinguishable from a legitimate node outage?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with duplicate NodeAccountID values mapping to different endpoints, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to silently overwrite earlier entries, reducing the effective network map and creating a partial partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause hiero.ClientForName(cfg.network) to return a client with a stale or incorrect node address book (e.g., via a cached DNS response) cause all subsequent submitWithRetry() calls to target unreachable nodes, inducing a network partition outside the design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing multicast or broadcast IP addresses, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a network map that routes consensus traffic to non-unicast addresses, inducing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause selective packet loss on the gRPC connection between the pinger and consensus nodes cause resp.GetReceipt(client) to block indefinitely (if no deadline is set), preventing the retry loop from advancing and effectively freezing the pinger in a partitioned state?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the pinger's container to run out of file descriptors (e.g., by exhausting /proc/sys/fs/file-max via a co-located workload) prevent os.WriteFile('/tmp/alive', ...) from opening the file, causing the liveness probe to fail and masking a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing IPv6 addresses when the pinger's network stack only supports IPv4, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a network map with unroutable endpoints and inducing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with a very large number of nodeEntry records (e.g., 100,000), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to spend excessive time building the network map, delaying pinger startup and creating a window during which the network is effectively partitioned?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the Hiero SDK's internal node selection algorithm to consistently pick the same unreachable node cause all cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) attempts to fail, even if other nodes are reachable, creating a partition that is caused by SDK behavior rather than actual network failure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the pinger's system clock to be skewed (e.g., via NTP poisoning) cause time.NewTicker(cfg.interval) to fire at incorrect intervals, disrupting the transfer cadence and causing the pinger to miss network partition detection windows?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope where GrpcProxyEndpoint points to an attacker-controlled TLS terminator, causing all gRPC traffic to be intercepted and selectively dropped, inducing a controlled network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can inject environment variables into the pinger's container (e.g., via a Kubernetes admission webhook or init container) set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a very large value (e.g., '24h'), causing the ticker to fire so infrequently that network partitions go undetected for extended periods?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to '0', causing submitWithRetry() to make only a single attempt per tick, so that any transient network partition immediately results in a logged failure with no retry-based recovery?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to a very large value (e.g., '1h'), causing the backoff() sleep between retries to be so long that the retry loop never completes within the ticker interval, effectively making the pinger unable to recover from any network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing the pinger's own IP address, causing the pinger to attempt gRPC connections to itself, creating a self-referential network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the Hiero network to return TRANSACTION_EXPIRED status on every receipt cause submitWithRetry() to treat this as a non-retryable error, exhausting all attempts and logging 'all attempts failed', creating an effective partition even when the network is reachable?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause repeated context cancellations (e.g., by sending SIGINT repeatedly) cause the pinger to restart in a loop, preventing it from ever completing a full transfer cycle and masking a network partition from monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing RFC 1918 private addresses that are not routable from the pinger's network segment, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a network map that induces a partition outside the design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with a Links.Next value pointing to an attacker-controlled URL, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to follow the pagination link and fetch adversarial node data, injecting a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the gRPC connection to consensus nodes to be throttled to near-zero bandwidth cause cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to consume the entire retry budget waiting for responses, so that the pinger never successfully completes a transfer and the network partition persists indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the pinger's /tmp filesystem to be mounted as read-only (e.g., via a Kubernetes securityContext or volume mount) prevent os.WriteFile('/tmp/ready', ...) from succeeding, causing the readiness probe to never pass and the pinger to never enter its transfer loop, creating a permanent startup-time partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing port 0, causing the Hiero SDK to attempt connections to an OS-assigned ephemeral port, inducing a network partition for all consensus node communication?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause hiero.ClientForName(cfg.network) to fail (e.g., by poisoning the SDK's embedded address book or causing a network lookup failure) prevent the pinger from initializing, causing log.Fatalf('client error') to terminate the process and create a permanent network partition from the monitoring perspective?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing hostnames that resolve to CNAME chains pointing to attacker-controlled infrastructure, causing the pinger to connect to adversarial nodes and operate on a partitioned network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the Hiero SDK to select a node that is behind a firewall blocking gRPC traffic cause cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to hang until the SDK's internal timeout, consuming the entire retry budget and creating a sustained network partition that is not detected by the liveness probe?
